situation: audit-2026-07-04-002 finding MM-M004 (priority 7): memory.rs L28+ MemoryDataProvider uses bare .read().unwrap() / .write().unwrap() on RwLock<HashMap<String, Quote>> at 5 sites (set_quote/set_quotes/all_quotes/reduce_available_volume/get_quote/list_symbols). If any holder thread panics while holding the lock, RwLock becomes poisoned and all subsequent access unwraps fail → engine actor cascade crash. Same class as already-merged MM-M001 (StrategyRegistry Mutex, commit 985aad9) and MM-M003 (sessions.rs 8 sites, commit 593f401).
action: Replaced all 5 unwrap() calls with unwrap_or_else(|e| { eprintln!("[moni-data/memory] RwLock poisoned on {read|write} ({site}), recovering: {e}"); e.into_inner() }) to recover from poisoned state and log warning instead of panicking. Pattern identical to MM-M001/MM-M003 fixes.
outcome_evidence: cargo check -p moni-data: Finished; cargo test -p moni-data: 7/7 passed; git commit 0fd703e in auto/opt-moni-data-1782919076; pushed to origin.
next_suggestion: Continue derived fix dispatch: next pending is MM-M005 (engine.rs L640 fill_quantity f64 precision loss, priority 8) or MM-M006 (engine.rs L424-433 attempt_match error path stale order, priority 9). Both are route-fix in moni-engine area.
